articles.title: Oppo Reno16 Pro global variant’s key specs and launch timeline tipped
articles.description: Last week, a tipster revealed the key specifications of the China-bound Oppo Reno16 Pro. Now, another source has shared details about the global variant, including its key specs and expected launch timeline. The device will succeed the global Reno15 Pro, which debuted in January this year. According to the tipster, the Reno16 Pro for global markets will be unveiled between July and August. Oppo Reno15 Pro (global) The tipster claims that the phone will come with a 6.32-inch OLED display, similar to its predecessor. It is said to offer a glass and aluminum build. The Reno16 Pro...

articles.title: Iran mocks Trump with shocking AI video showing 'successful strikes' on America's Mid-East base |War
articles.description: Iran’s war messaging has entered a new phase of digital spectacle. The official X handle of the Consulate General of Iran in Mumbai released an AI-generated video on April 29 that appeared to depict Iranian fighter jets striking a U.S. base in Kuwait, while boasting that Iranian precision had outmatched American forces and mocking the U.S. Air Force for allegedly downing its own aircraft.

articles.title: Rumored Apple Vision Pro team break-up isn't a death knell for the product
articles.description: A new rumor suggests Apple Vision Pro hardware may be dead, but the dissolution of a team doesn't necessarily mean that pipeline is dead. If anything, it's business as usual. Apple Vision Pro isn't dead Whenever Apple releases a new product category, there seems to be this industry drive to find its weak points and jab at it until it dies . Apple Vision Pro may not be a blockbuster, but it is the entry point to Spatial Computing, which Apple still believes to be its future . According to a report from MacRumors , Apple Vision Pro hardware as it stands in April 2026 may truly be dead. The story suggests that Apple has likely given up on the platform due to a lack of consumer interest after the M5 update. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums

articles.title: STL delivers resilient FY'26 performance; AI Data Centre business emerges as key growth engine
articles.description: Mumbai (Maharashtra) [India], April 29: STL (NSE: STLTECH), a leading connectivity solutions provider for AI-ready digital infrastructure, today announced its financial results for the year ended 31 March, 2026. The Company reported revenues of INR 1,441 Cr for the quarter and INR 4,745 Cr for FY26, a growth of ~14.7% QoQ and 18.8% YoY. In FY26, STL delivered EBITDA margins at ~13.2% and EBITDA of INR 628 Cr. The year was characterised by a transformative ~110% surge in order intake over FY25. This indicates strong revenue visibility and sets the stage for sustained growth in the coming quarters.

articles.title: Who is Xu Zewei, alleged Chinese hacker extradited by the FBI from Italy?
articles.description: The FBI has brought alleged China-linked hacker Xu Zewei to the United States. FBI Director Kash Patel stated his recent Italy trip was vital for this rare extradition. Xu faces federal charges for a cyber campaign targeting US COVID-19 research. Patel confirmed Xu's direct ties to China's Ministry of State Security. This operation highlights ongoing efforts against Chinese espionage.

articles.description: Google's upcoming Tensor G6 chipset for Pixel 11 phones shows a CPU upgrade with new ARM cores, promising faster single-core performance. However, a leaked GPU specification suggests a step back to older PowerVR technology, potentially impacting gaming. The Pixel 11 series, codenamed Cubs, is expected in August.

articles.title: Apple may finally give iOS 27's Photos app the AI editing tools Google and Samsung users have had for years
articles.description: Apple is reportedly adding three new AI photo editing tools—Extend, Enhance, and Reframe—to the Photos app in iOS 27, iPadOS 27, and macOS 27 this fall. The features, powered by Apple Intelligence, will process entirely on-device and join the existing Clean Up tool. It's a long-overdue catch-up with Google and Samsung, though Extend and Reframe are already showing development trouble ahead of the June 8 WWDC preview.

articles.title: Unix UX-W3 Havana headset review: Gets the job done
articles.description: The Unix Havana headset, priced at Rs 1,699, offers a blend of gaming features and daily usability with its long battery life and RGB lighting. It provides decent audio for casual listening and calls, with effective noise reduction for clearer conversations. Comfortable for extended wear, it's a practical choice for casual users and light gaming.

articles.title: How King Gillette Turned a Simple Idea Into a Shaving Revolution
articles.description: King C. Gillette transformed shaving by introducing the disposable blade safety razor. This innovation solved a daily annoyance for men. It offered a simpler, more convenient way to shave. Gillette's genius lay in creating a product that required repeat purchases of blades. This business model became a blueprint for many industries.
